configuration.autoCenterCommitDetailsVie: Automatically center the commit details view when it is opened.
Depreciated: This setting has been renamed to git-graph.commitDetailsView.autoCenter
configuration.combineLocalAndRemoteBranc: Combine local and remote branch labels if they refer to the same branch, and are on the same commit.
Depreciated: This setting has been renamed to git-graph.referenceLabels.combineLocalAndRemoteBranchLabels
configuration.commitDetailsView.autoCent: Automatically center the commit details view when it is opened.
configuration.commitDetailsView.autoScro: Automatically scroll the view to display the commit when the commit details view is opened.
configuration.commitDetailsView.fileView: Render the file tree in the commit details view in a compact form, compressing folders with only one subfolder into a single combined folder element.
Set the default file view type used in the commit details view. This can be overridden per repository using the controls on the right side of the commit details view.
Display files in a list (useful for repositories with deep folder structures).
Display files in a tree structure.
configuration.commitDetailsView.location: Specify the location where the commit details view is rendered in the Git Graph view.
Show the commit details view docked to the bottom of the Git Graph view.
Show the commit details view inline with the graph and commits.
configuration.commitDetailsViewFileTreeC: Render the File Tree in the Commit Details / Comparison View in a compacted form, such that folders with a single child folder are compressed into a single combined folder element.
Depreciated: This setting has been renamed to git-graph.commitDetailsView.fileView.fileTree.compactFolders
configuration.commitDetailsViewLocation: Specifies where the Commit Details View is rendered in the Git Graph view.
Depreciated: This setting has been renamed to git-graph.commitDetailsView.location
configuration.commitDetailsViewShowStage: Show staged changes in the commit details view.
Depreciated: This setting has been renamed to git-graph.commitDetailsView.showStagedChanges
configuration.commitOrdering: Specifies the order of commits on the Git Graph view. See [git log](https://git-scm.com/docs/git-log#_commit_ordering) for more information on each order option.
configuration.commitOrdering.deprecation: Depreciated: This setting has been renamed to git-graph.repository.commits.order
configuration.contextMenuActionsVisibili: Customise which context menu actions are visible. For example, if you want to hide the rebase action from the branch context menu, the appropriate value for this setting is { "branch": { "rebase": false } }. For more information on how to configure this setting, check out the documentation [here](https://github.com/hansu/vscode-git-graph/wiki/Extension-Settings#context-menu-actions-visibility).
Rebase Current Branch onto This Commit...
Reset Current Branch to This Commit...
Copy Absolute File Path to Clipboard
Copy Relative File Path to Clipboard
configuration.customBranchGlobPatterns: Array of custom branch glob patterns to display in the 'Branches' dropdown. Example: [{"name": "Feature Requests", "glob": "heads/feature/*"}]
configuration.customBranchGlobPatterns.i: Glob pattern <glob-pattern> as used in 'git log --glob=<glob-pattern>'. For example: heads/feature/*
Name used to refer to the pattern in the 'Branches' dropdown
configuration.customEmojiShortcodeMappin: Array of custom emoji shortcode mappings. Example: [{"shortcode": ":sparkles:", "emoji":"✨"}]
configuration.customPullRequestProviders: Array of custom pull request providers that can be used in the 'Pull Request Creation' integration. For information on how to configure this setting, see the documentation [here](https://github.com/hansu/vscode-git-graph/wiki/Configuring-a-custom-Pull-Request-Provider).
The unique identifying display name for the provider.

Template URL that can be used to create a pull request, after replacing $1 - $8 variables to build the final URL. For information on how to configure this setting, see the documentation [here](https://github.com/hansu/vscode-git-graph/wiki/Configuring-a-custom-Pull-Request-Provider).
configuration.date.format: Specify the date format used in the 'Date' column of the Git Graph view.
configuration.date.type: Specify the date type displayed in the 'Date' column of the Git Graph view.
Use the author date of the commit.
Use the committer date of the commit.
configuration.dateFormat: Specifies the date format to be used in the "Date" column on the Git Graph View.
configuration.dateFormat.deprecationMess: Depreciated: This setting has been renamed to git-graph.date.format
configuration.dateType: Specifies the date type to be displayed in the "Date" column on the Git Graph View.
configuration.dateType.deprecationMessag: Depreciated: This setting has been renamed to git-graph.date.type
configuration.defaultColumnVisibility: Object specifying the default visibility of the Date, Author, and Commit columns. Example: {"Date": true, "Author": true, "Commit": true}
configuration.defaultFileViewType: Sets the default type of File View used in the Commit Details / Comparison Views. This can be overridden per repository using the controls on the right side of the Commit Details / Comparison Views.
configuration.defaultFileViewType.deprec: Depreciated: This setting has been renamed to git-graph.commitDetailsView.fileView.type
configuration.defaultViewMode: Specifies the default view mode when opening Git Graph.
configuration.defaultViewMode.deprecatio: Depreciated: This setting has been removed. The Git Graph View now always opens in commits view mode.
configuration.defaultViewMode.enumDescri: Show only branches when opening Git Graph
Show all commits when opening Git Graph
configuration.dialog.addTag.pushToRemote: Default state of the field indicating whether to push the tag to the remote after adding it.
configuration.dialog.addTag.type: Default type of tag to add.
configuration.dialog.applyStash.reinstat: Default state of the "Reinstate Index" checkbox.
configuration.dialog.cherryPick.noCommit: Default state of the "No Commit" checkbox.
configuration.dialog.cherryPick.recordOr: Default state of the "Record Origin" checkbox.
configuration.dialog.deleteBranch.forceD: Default state of the "Force Delete" checkbox.
configuration.dialog.fetchIntoLocalBranc: Default state of the "Force Fetch" checkbox.
configuration.dialog.fetchRemote.pruneTa: Default state of the "Prune Tags" checkbox.
configuration.dialog.general.referenceIn: Specify the substitution to automatically perform when space characters are entered or pasted into reference inputs in dialogs (e.g. creating branches, adding tags, etc.).
Replace space characters with hyphens, e.g.: "new branch" -> "new-branch".
Replace space characters with underscores, e.g.: "new branch" -> "new_branch".
configuration.dialog.merge.allowUnrelate: Default state of the "Allow unrelated histories" checkbox.
configuration.dialog.merge.noCommit: Default state of the "No Commit" checkbox.
configuration.dialog.merge.noFastForward: Default state of the "Create a new commit even if fast-forward is possible" checkbox.
configuration.dialog.merge.squashCommits: Default state of the "Squash Commits" checkbox.
configuration.dialog.merge.squashMessage: Specify the message format used for squashed commits (when the "Squash Commits" option is selected).
Use the Git Graph generated squash message.
Use the Git generated detailed squash message (stored in .git/SQUASH_MSG).
configuration.dialog.popStash.reinstateI: Default state of the "Reinstate Index" checkbox.
configuration.dialog.pullBranch.noFastFo: Default state of the "Create a new commit even if fast-forward is possible" checkbox.
configuration.dialog.pullBranch.squashCo: Default state of the "Squash Commits" checkbox.
configuration.dialog.pullBranch.squashMe: Specify the message format used for squashed commits (when the "Squash Commits" option is selected).
Use the Git Graph generated squash message.
Use the Git generated detailed squash message (stored in .git/SQUASH_MSG).
configuration.dialog.rebase.ignoreDate: Default state of the "Ignore Date (non-interactive rebase only)" checkbox.
configuration.dialog.rebase.launchIntera: Default state of the "Interactive Rebase (launched in new terminal)" checkbox.
configuration.dialog.resetCurrentBranchT: Default mode to use for reset operations.
Mixed - Keep working tree, but reset index
Soft - Keep all changes, but reset HEAD
configuration.dialog.resetUncommittedCha: Default mode to use for reset operations.
Mixed - Keep working tree, but reset index
configuration.dialog.stashUncommittedCha: Default state of the "Include Untracked Files" checkbox.
configuration.enhancedAccessibility: Visual file change A|M|D|R|U indicators in the Commit Details View for users with colour blindness. In the future, this setting will enable any additional accessibility related features of Git Graph that aren't enabled by default.
configuration.fetchAndPrune: Before fetching from remote(s) using the Fetch button on the Git Graph View Control Bar, remove any remote-tracking references that no longer exist on the remote(s).
configuration.fetchAndPrune.deprecationM: Depreciated: This setting has been renamed to git-graph.repository.fetchAndPrune
configuration.fetchAvatars: Fetch avatars of commit authors and committers. By enabling this setting, you consent to commit author and committer email addresses being sent GitHub, GitLab or Gravatar, depending on the repositories remote origin.
configuration.fetchAvatars.deprecationMe: Depreciated: This setting has been renamed to git-graph.repository.commits.fetchAvatars
configuration.fileEncoding: The character set encoding used when retrieving a specific version of repository files (e.g. in the Diff View). A list of all supported encodings can be found [here](https://github.com/ashtuchkin/iconv-lite/wiki/Supported-Encodings).
configuration.graph.colours: Specifies the colours used on the graph.
configuration.graph.colours.item.descrip: Each array item is a CSS colour: #RRGGBB, #RRGGBBAA, rgb(r,g,b), or rgba(r,g,b,a).
configuration.graph.style: Specifies the style of the graph.
configuration.graph.style.enumDescriptio: Use angular lines when transitioning between branches on the graph.
Use smooth curves when transitioning between branches on the graph.
configuration.graph.uncommittedChanges: Specifies how the Uncommitted Changes are displayed on the graph.
configuration.graph.uncommittedChanges.e: Open Circle at the Checked Out Commit
Open Circle at the Uncommitted Changes

Display the Uncommitted Changes as a grey closed circle, connected to the commit referenced by HEAD with a dotted grey line. The commit referenced by HEAD is therefore always displayed as an open circle.

Display the Uncommitted Changes as a grey open circle, connected to the commit referenced by HEAD with a solid grey line. The current file system's state is therefore always displayed as an open circle.
configuration.graphColours: Specifies the colours used on the graph.
configuration.graphColours.deprecationMe: Depreciated: This setting has been renamed to git-graph.graph.colours
configuration.graphShowMergeCommitsAsSqu: Display merge commits as squares in the git graph.
Depreciated: This setting has been renamed to git-graph.graph.showMergeCommitsAsSquares
configuration.graphStyle: Specifies the style of the graph.
configuration.graphStyle.deprecationMess: Depreciated: This setting has been renamed to git-graph.graph.style
configuration.graphStyle.enumDescription: Use angular lines when transitioning between branches on the graph
Use smooth curves when transitioning between branches on the graph
configuration.includeAllCommitsFromAllBr: When viewing specific branches, include all commits that are ancestors of those branches (instead of only the commits on those branches).
Depreciated: This setting has been removed. Use the 'Include All Commits' button in the Git Graph View's Control Bar instead.
configuration.includeCommitsMentionedByR: Include commits only mentioned by reflogs in the Git Graph View (only applies when showing all branches). This can be overridden per repository in the Git Graph View's Repository Settings Widget.
Depreciated: This setting has been renamed to git-graph.repository.includeCommitsMentionedByReflogs
configuration.initialLoadCommits: Specifies the number of commits to initially load.
configuration.initialLoadCommits.depreca: Depreciated: This setting has been renamed to git-graph.repository.commits.initialLoad
configuration.integratedTerminalShell: Specifies the path and filename of the Shell executable to be used by the Visual Studio Code Integrated Terminal, when it is opened by Git Graph. For example, to use Git Bash on Windows this setting would commonly be set to "C:\Program Files\Git\bin\bash.exe". If this setting is left blank, the default Shell is used.
configuration.keyboardShortcut.find: The keybinding for the keyboard shortcut that opens the Find Widget in the Git Graph View.
configuration.keyboardShortcut.refresh: The keybinding for the keyboard shortcut that refreshes the Git Graph View.
configuration.keyboardShortcut.scrollToH: The keybinding for the keyboard shortcut that scrolls the Git Graph View to be centered on the commit referenced by HEAD.
configuration.keyboardShortcut.scrollToS: The keybinding for the keyboard shortcut that scrolls the Git Graph View to the first (or next) stash in the loaded commits. The Shift Key Modifier can be applied to this keybinding to scroll the Git Graph View to the last (or previous) stash in the loaded commits.
configuration.language: Set the display language for Git Graph.
configuration.loadMoreCommits: Specifies the number of additional commits to load when the "Load More Commits" button is pressed, or more commits are automatically loaded.
configuration.loadMoreCommits.deprecatio: Depreciated: This setting has been renamed to git-graph.repository.commits.loadMore
configuration.loadMoreCommitsAutomatical: When the view has been scrolled to the bottom, automatically load more commits if they exist (instead of having to press the "Load More Commits" button).
Depreciated: This setting has been renamed to git-graph.repository.commits.loadMoreAutomatically
configuration.markdown: Parse and render a frequently used subset of inline Markdown formatting rules in commit messages and tag details (bold, italics, bold & italics, and inline code blocks).
configuration.maxDepthOfRepoSearch: Specifies the maximum depth of subfolders to search when discovering repositories in the workspace. Note: Sub-repos are not automatically detected when searching subfolders, however they can be manually added by running the command "Git Graph: Add Git Repository" in the Command Palette.
configuration.muteCommitsThatAreNotAnces: Display commits that aren't ancestors of the checked-out branch / commit with a muted text color. Muting will only occur if the commit referenced by HEAD is within the loaded commits on the Git Graph View.
Depreciated: This setting has been renamed to git-graph.repository.commits.mute.commitsThatAreNotAncestorsOfHead
configuration.muteMergeCommits: Display merge commits with a muted text color.
configuration.muteMergeCommits.deprecati: Depreciated: This setting has been renamed to git-graph.repository.commits.mute.mergeCommits
configuration.onlyFollowFirstParent: Only follow the first parent of commits when discovering the commits to load in the Git Graph View. See [--first-parent](https://git-scm.com/docs/git-log#Documentation/git-log.txt---first-parent) to find out more about this setting. This can be overridden per repository in the Git Graph View's Repository Settings Widget.
configuration.onlyFollowFirstParent.depr: Depreciated: This setting has been renamed to git-graph.repository.onlyFollowFirstParent
configuration.openDiffTabLocation: Specifies which Editor Group the Visual Studio Code Diff View is opened in.
configuration.openDiffTabLocation.deprec: Depreciated: This setting has been renamed to git-graph.openNewTabEditorGroup
configuration.openDiffTabLocation.enumDe: Open the Visual Studio Code Diff View in the Active Editor Group.
Open the Visual Studio Code Diff View beside the Active Editor Group.
Open the Visual Studio Code Diff View in the Eighth Editor Group.
Open the Visual Studio Code Diff View in the Fifth Editor Group.

Open the Visual Studio Code Diff View in the Seventh Editor Group.
Open the Visual Studio Code Diff View in the Sixth Editor Group.
Open the Visual Studio Code Diff View in the Third Editor Group.
Open the Visual Studio Code Diff View in the Second Editor Group.
configuration.openNewTabEditorGroup: Specifies the Editor Group where Git Graph should open new tabs, when performing the following actions from the Git Graph View: Viewing the Visual Studio Code Diff View, Opening a File, Viewing a File at a Specific Revision.

configuration.openRepoToHead: When opening or switching repositories in the Git Graph View, automatically scroll the view to be centered on the commit referenced by HEAD. This will only occur if the commit referenced by HEAD is within the loaded commits on the Git Graph View.
configuration.openRepoToHead.deprecation: Depreciated: This setting has been renamed to git-graph.repository.onLoad.scrollToHead
configuration.openToTheRepoOfTheActiveTe: Open the Git Graph View to the repository containing the active Text Editor document.
configuration.referenceLabelAlignment: Specifies how branch and tag reference labels are aligned for each commit.
configuration.referenceLabelAlignment.de: Depreciated: This setting has been renamed to git-graph.referenceLabels.alignment
configuration.referenceLabelAlignment.en: Branches (aligned to the graph) & Tags (on the right)
Branches (on the left) & Tags (on the right)
Show branch labels aligned to the graph in the 'Graph' column, and tag labels on the right in the 'Description' column.
Show branch labels on the left of the commit message in the 'Description' column, and tag labels on the right.
Show branch & tag labels on the left of the commit message in the 'Description' column.
configuration.referenceLabels.alignment: Specifies how branch and tag reference labels are aligned for each commit.
Branches (aligned to the graph) & Tags (on the right)
Branches (on the left) & Tags (on the right)
Show branch labels aligned to the graph in the 'Graph' column, and tag labels on the right in the 'Description' column.
Show branch labels on the left of the commit message in the 'Description' column, and tag labels on the right.
Show branch & tag labels on the left of the commit message in the 'Description' column.
configuration.referenceLabels.combineLoc: Combine local and remote branch labels if they refer to the same branch, and are on the same commit.
configuration.repository.commits.fetchAv: Fetch avatars of commit authors and committers. By enabling this setting, you consent to commit author and committer email addresses being sent GitHub, GitLab or Gravatar, depending on the repositories remote origin.
configuration.repository.commits.initial: Specifies the number of commits to initially load.
configuration.repository.commits.loadMor: Specifies the number of additional commits to load when the "Load More Commits" button is pressed, or more commits are automatically loaded.
When the view has been scrolled to the bottom, automatically load more commits if they exist (instead of having to press the "Load More Commits" button).
configuration.repository.commits.mute.co: Display commits that aren't ancestors of the checked-out branch / commit with a muted text color. Muting will only occur if the commit referenced by HEAD is within the loaded commits on the Git Graph View.
configuration.repository.commits.mute.me: Display merge commits with a muted text color.
configuration.repository.commits.order: Specifies the order of commits on the Git Graph View. See [git log](https://git-scm.com/docs/git-log#_commit_ordering) for more information on each order option. This can be overridden per repository via the Git Graph View's Column Header Context Menu.
configuration.repository.commits.order.e: Show commits in the author timestamp order.
Show commits in the commit timestamp order.
Avoid showing commits on multiple lines of history intermixed.
configuration.repository.commits.showSig: Show the commit's signature status to the right of the Committer in the Commit Details View (only for signed commits). Hovering over the signature icon displays a tooltip with the signature details. Requires Git (>= 2.4.0) & GPG (or equivalent) to be installed on the same machine that is running Visual Studio Code.
configuration.repository.fetchAndPrune: Before fetching from remote(s) using the Fetch button on the Git Graph View Control Bar, remove any remote-tracking references that no longer exist on the remote(s).
configuration.repository.fetchAndPruneTa: Before fetching from remote(s) using the Fetch button on the Git Graph View Control Bar, remove any local tags that no longer exist on the remote(s). Requires Git >= 2.17.0, and the "Repository: Fetch And Prune" setting to be enabled. Caution: If you work in repositories that have multiple remotes, it is not recommended to use this setting (instead you can prune tags for a specific remote via "Fetch Remote" Dialog from the Repository Settings Widget on the Git Graph View).
configuration.repository.includeCommitsM: Include commits only mentioned by reflogs in the Git Graph View (only applies when showing all branches). This can be overridden per repository in the Git Graph View's Repository Settings Widget.
configuration.repository.onLoad.scrollTo: Automatically scroll the Git Graph View to be centered on the commit referenced by HEAD. This will only occur if the commit referenced by HEAD is within the loaded commits on the Git Graph View.
configuration.repository.onLoad.showChec: Show the checked out branch when a repository is loaded in the Git Graph View. This setting can be used in conjunction with "Repository > On Load: Show Specific Branches". Default: false (show all branches)
configuration.repository.onLoad.showSpec: Show specific branches when a repository is loaded in the Git Graph View. Branches can be specified as follows: A local branch name (e.g. `master`), a remote-tracking branch name prefixed with "remotes/" (e.g. `remotes/origin/master`), or a glob pattern defined in `git-graph.customBranchGlobPatterns` prefixed with "--glob=" (e.g. `--glob=heads/feature/*`). This setting can be used in conjunction with "Repository > On Load: Show Checked Out Branch". Default: [] (show all branches)
configuration.repository.onlyFollowFirst: Only follow the first parent of commits when discovering the commits to load in the Git Graph View. See [--first-parent](https://git-scm.com/docs/git-log#Documentation/git-log.txt---first-parent) to find out more about this setting. This can be overridden per repository in the Git Graph View's Repository Settings Widget.
configuration.repository.showCommitsOnly: Show Commits that are only referenced by tags in Git Graph.
configuration.repository.showRemoteBranc: Show Remote Branches in Git Graph by default. This can be overridden per repository from the Git Graph View's Control Bar.
configuration.repository.showRemoteHeads: Show Remote HEAD Symbolic References in Git Graph (e.g. "origin/HEAD").
configuration.repository.showStashes: Show Stashes in Git Graph by default. This can be overridden per repository in the Git Graph View's Repository Settings Widget.
configuration.repository.showTags: Show Tags in Git Graph by default. This can be overridden per repository in the Git Graph View's Repository Settings Widget.
configuration.repository.showUncommitted: Show uncommitted changes. If you work on large repositories, disabling this setting can reduce the load time of the Git Graph View.
configuration.repository.showUntrackedFi: Show untracked files when viewing the uncommitted changes. If you work on large repositories, disabling this setting can reduce the load time of the Git Graph View.
configuration.repository.sign.commits: Enables commit signing with GPG or X.509.
configuration.repository.sign.tags: Enables tag signing with GPG or X.509.
configuration.repository.simplifyByDecor: Show branch topology by omitting commits that are not referenced by tags. This can be overridden per repository from the Git Graph View's Control Bar.
configuration.repository.singleAuthorSel: Enables the selection of only a single author from the Authors dropdown. Multiple authors can be selected by 'Ctrl' (or 'Cmd' on macOS) + click.
configuration.repository.singleBranchSel: Enables the selection of only a single branch from the Branches dropdown. Multiple branches can be selected by 'Ctrl' (or 'Cmd' on macOS) + click.
configuration.repository.useMailmap: Respect [.mailmap](https://git-scm.com/docs/git-check-mailmap#_mapping_authors) files when displaying author & committer names and email addresses.
configuration.repositoryDropdownOrder: Specifies the order that repositories are sorted in the repository dropdown on the Git Graph View (only visible when more than one repository exists in the current Visual Studio Code Workspace).
configuration.repositoryDropdownOrder.en: Sort repositories alphabetically by the full path of the repository.
Sort repositories alphabetically by the name of the repository.
Sort repositories according to the Visual Studio Code Workspace Folder order, then alphabetically by the full path of the repository.
configuration.retainContextWhenHidden: Specifies if the Git Graph View's Visual Studio Code context is kept when the panel is no longer visible (e.g. moved to background tab). Enabling this setting will make Git Graph load significantly faster when switching back to the Git Graph tab, however has a higher memory overhead.
configuration.showBranchLabels: Show branch labels in the git graph.
configuration.showBranchLabels.deprecati: Depreciated: This setting has been renamed to git-graph.referenceLabels.showBranches
configuration.showCommitsOnlyReferencedB: Show commits that are only referenced by tags in Git Graph.
Depreciated: This setting has been renamed to git-graph.repository.showCommitsOnlyReferencedByTags
configuration.showCommitsWithNoParents: Show commits that have no parents (e.g. initial commit).
configuration.showCurrentBranchAtTop: When viewing a specific branch, show the commits on that branch at the top of the Git Graph View (instead of being spread across the view)
configuration.showCurrentBranchByDefault: Show the current branch by default when Git Graph is opened. Default: false (show all branches)
Depreciated: This setting has been renamed to git-graph.repository.onLoad.showCheckedOutBranch
configuration.showSignatureStatus: Show the commit's signature status to the right of the Committer in the Commit Details View (only for signed commits). Hovering over the signature icon displays a tooltip with the signature details. Requires Git (>= 2.4.0) & GPG (or equivalent) to be installed on the same machine that is running Visual Studio Code.
configuration.showSignatureStatus.deprec: Depreciated: This setting has been renamed to git-graph.repository.commits.showSignatureStatus
configuration.showStatusBarItem: Show a Status Bar Item that opens the Git Graph View when clicked.
configuration.showTagLabels: Show tag labels in the git graph.
configuration.showTagLabels.deprecationM: Depreciated: This setting has been renamed to git-graph.referenceLabels.showTags
configuration.showTags: Show Tags in Git Graph by default. This can be overridden per repository in the Git Graph View's Repository Settings Widget.
configuration.showTags.deprecationMessag: Depreciated: This setting has been renamed to git-graph.repository.showTags
configuration.showUncommittedChanges: Show uncommitted changes. If you work on large repositories, disabling this setting can reduce the load time of the Git Graph View.
configuration.showUncommittedChanges.dep: Depreciated: This setting has been renamed to git-graph.repository.showUncommittedChanges
configuration.showUntrackedFiles: Show untracked files when viewing the uncommitted changes. If you work on large repositories, disabling this setting can reduce the load time of the Git Graph View.
configuration.showUntrackedFiles.depreca: Depreciated: This setting has been renamed to git-graph.repository.showUntrackedFiles
configuration.sourceCodeProviderIntegrat: Specifies where the "View Git Graph" action appears on the title of SCM Providers.
Show the 'View Git Graph' action on the title of SCM Providers
Show the 'View Git Graph' action in the 'More Actions...' menu on the title of SCM Providers
configuration.stashChangesBeforeCheckout: When checking out a branch that is not the current branch, automatically stash uncommitted changes in the current branch.
configuration.stickyHeader: Keeps the header visible when the view is scrolled
configuration.syncScrollingOfCommitDetai: Enable vertical scrolling of the Commit Details View to be synchronized with the Git Graph View.
configuration.tabIconColourTheme: Specifies the colour theme of the icon displayed on the Git Graph tab.
configuration.tabIconColourTheme.enumDes: Show a colour icon which suits most Visual Studio Code colour themes
Show a grey icon which suits Visual Studio Code colour themes that are predominantly grayscale
configuration.telemetry: Allow anonymous usage data to be collected.
configuration.terminal: Specifies which terminal to use for Git commands (e.g. interactive rebase, merge, etc.).
configuration.terminal.enumDescription.e: Use an external terminal for Git commands.
configuration.terminal.enumDescription.i: Use the integrated terminal for Git commands.
configuration.timezone: Specifies the timezone to display commit & tag timestamps in.
configuration.timezone.enumDescription.l: Show commit & tag timestamps in the local timezone.
configuration.timezone.enumDescription.u: Show commit & tag timestamps in UTC.
configuration.toolbarButtonVisibility: Object specifying the default visibility of toolbar items. Example: {"Remotes": true, "Simplify": true}
configuration.useMailmap: Respect [.mailmap](https://git-scm.com/docs/git-check-mailmap#_mapping_authors) files when displaying author & committer names and email addresses.
configuration.useMailmap.deprecationMess: Depreciated: This setting has been renamed to git-graph.repository.useMailmap
configuration.useUnicodeCharacters: Use Unicode characters to draw the graph. Disabling this setting will use ASCII characters instead.
configuration.useUnicodeCharacters.depre: Depreciated: This setting has been renamed to git-graph.graph.useUnicodeCharacters
configuration.viewLocation: Specifies where to display the Git Graph view by default.
configuration.viewLocation.enumDescripti: Allow both display modes (use specific commands to open in each location)
Display Git Graph in the editor area (traditional view)
Display Git Graph in the panel area (bottom/side panel)
configuration.workspace: Specifies the workspace mode to use.
Allow multiple repositories to be opened at once (one repository per workspace folder).
Allow only one repository to be opened at once.
extension.description: Fork of mhutchie's Git Graph with further improvements.
extension.name: Git Graph 2

ui.andUseTheFileSystemsDefaultName: and use the file system's default name
